description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared metal horn with a blue-green patina and red-orange decorative accents. The horn is attached to a curved, copper-colored metal arm that connects to a dark wooden base with ornate paneling and a visible winding handle. On top of the base sits a black turntable platter with a vinyl record, and a metallic tonearm positioned over the record. The base is rectangular and appears to be made of polished wood, with a small metallic latch or knob on the front. The overall design is ornate and classic, evoking early 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large blue-green horn, ornate wooden base, and visible tonearm over a black platter.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved copper arm, large flared horn, and the record on the platter.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the horn's opening, the tonearm, and the winding handle on the left side of the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn and arm, showing the record and the base's decorative paneling.
description_view_3: Direct view into the horn, emphasizing its blue-green and red pattern, with the base and platter partially visible below.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and circular, black in color, and occupies the top center of the wooden base. It is slightly raised and supports a vinyl record, with a visible central spindle. The platter appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a matte finish and subtle grooves.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and copper-colored, with a curved shape extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is of medium length and appears to be resting on the record, with a visible joint at the base for movement.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its location is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
attribute_control_knobs: A single winding handle is visible on the left side of the base, metallic in appearance, with a rounded end. No other control knobs or buttons are visible.
